purpose: Maps the progression of sovereign stress from early warning through crisis, providing real-time placement of sovereigns along a six-stage continuum.
components: Stage 1: Fiscal arithmetic deterioration (rising debt service, WAC rollover, widening comprehensive vs headline deficit gap)
Stage 2: Market signal emergence (term premium expansion, correlation bifurcation, auction tail widening, cross-asset inconsistency)
Stage 3: Policy constraint binding (rate ceiling activation, forward guidance degradation, Taylor coefficient compression)
Stage 4: Transmission mechanism stress (basis trade fragility, demand composition tipping, sovereign-corporate channel loading)
Stage 5: Institutional capitulation (~300-400bp spread widening from European crisis template)
Stage 6: Crisis resolution (financial repression, restructuring, inflation erosion, or positive-tail productivity resolution)
current_assessment: US placed at Stage 1-2: fiscal arithmetic deteriorating (WAC rollover underway), early market signals emerging (correlation bifurcation, auction tail widening), but policy constraints not yet binding at current rate levels.
limitations: Stage progression is not necessarily linear or monotonic — stages can be skipped or reversed
N=2-3 for full progression in advanced economies
Individual stages can persist for years without advancing
External shocks (geopolitical, pandemic) can accelerate or decelerate progression
The framework does not assign probabilities to progression between stages
